node.statement: (NPA hierarchy as constrained state space). At level k of the NPA hierarchy, one considers moment matrices Γ = (φ(s_i* s_j))_{i,j} indexed by words s_i of length ≤ k in noncommuting operator variables. The constraint Γ ≽ 0 ensures φ extends to a positive functional on the *-algebra generated by the operators. Additional linear constraints encode the measurement structure (commutation of operators from different parties, projector relations, etc.). At convergence, feasible points correspond to states φ on a finitely-presented *-algebra A_k subject to m_k affine constraints, where m_k is the number of linear constraints at level k.
